name: Coconut Angel Food Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the cake flour onto a sheet of wax paper three times.
2. In a large bowl with an electric mixer, beat the egg whites with salt until frothy.
3. Add cream of tartar and beat to soft peaks.
4. Beat in half cup sugar gradually, then add vanilla and almond extract.
5. Continue beating until stiff peaks form. Fold in the remaining sugar gradually.
6. Sift flour over egg whites in four batches, folding gently after each sifting.
7. Fold in coconut.
8. Spoon batter into a 9-inch tube pan, 3-1/2 inches deep.
9. Bake at 275°F for 1-1/2 hours in the center of a preheated oven.
10. Hang the cake upside down on a bottle neck and let cool for 90-120 minutes or until completely cooled.
11. Turn out the cake onto a rack, invert it onto a plate.
12. In a metal bowl, whisk sugar, egg whites, zest, juice, corn syrup, and a pinch of salt over simmering water.
13. Cook until the mixture reaches 140°F on a candy thermometer.
14. Remove from heat and whisk over hot water for an additional 3 minutes.
15. Beat frosting with an electric mixer until cool and holds stiff peaks.
16. Spread frosting over top and sides, coating with toasted coconut.
1977 Gourmet Magazine favorite
